name: People'S Alliance
description_short: ALLIANCE Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative headquartered in Lubbock, Texas, offering comprehensive banking, lending, and mortgage services.
description_long:
ALLIANCE Credit Union operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative based in Lubbock, Texas. The organization was established to serve consumer financial needs with a cooperative business model emphasizing member value over corporate profit. With multiple branch locations across the Lubbock area and a 24/7 ALLIANCE Express ITM drive-thru service, the credit union provides accessible banking to its membership base.

ALLIANCE offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including personal checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit, IRAs, and multiple loan products. Their lending portfolio includes auto loans, student loans, home mortgages (conventional, FHA, VA, USDA, construction, and self-employed options), home equity lines of credit, and commercial loans. The credit union also issues Visa credit cards, provides refinancing options, and offers specialty products like their Bits of Stock™ program and skip-a-pay loan options.

As a credit union, ALLIANCE distinguishes itself through its not-for-profit structure where profits are reinvested into member services rather than distributed to shareholders. The organization emphasizes competitive loan rates and deposit yields that exceed typical commercial bank offerings. They maintain active fraud prevention resources, cybersecurity education, and identity theft protection measures. The credit union also engages in community service through the ACU Foundation and provides financial education resources including calculators and articles for members.

ALLIANCE Credit Union is best suited for consumers in the Lubbock, Texas area seeking traditional banking and lending services with the added benefit of member ownership and not-for-profit governance. The primary limitation is geographic availability—services appear concentrated in West Texas. Like all credit unions, membership eligibility may apply, though the website indicates they actively seek new members. For those within their service area, ALLIANCE represents a cooperative alternative to commercial banks with emphasis on member financial well-being. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts (ALLIANCE Checking and Revive Checking products)
Savings accounts and money market accounts
Certificates of deposit and junior certificates
Auto loans and auto refinancing with skip-a-pay options
Student loans and parent loans with refinancing
Mortgage products (conventional, FHA, VA, USDA, construction, self-employed, H.O.M.E. 100)
Home equity lines of credit (HELOC) and second lien mortgages
Commercial checking and commercial loan options
Visa credit cards (Visa Advantage and Visa Secured)
Renovation loans and commercial renovation financing
pros: Member-owned not-for-profit structure reinvests profits back into services rather than shareholder returns
Offers comprehensive mortgage options including FHA, VA, USDA, construction, and self-employed home loans
24/7 ALLIANCE Express ITM drive-thru service for after-hours banking access
Competitive loan rates and higher deposit rates than competitors, according to their stated business model
Active fraud prevention and cybersecurity resources with dedicated identity theft and lost card support
Diverse loan products including auto, student, personal, home equity, and commercial options
Multiple branch locations with extended hours (7:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. weekdays)
cons: Geographic service area appears limited to Lubbock, Texas region—not available nationwide
No specific information provided about membership eligibility requirements or restrictions
Website does not disclose APR ranges, fees, or specific rate comparisons to validate competitive claims
No mention of digital banking capabilities, mobile app features, or online-only account opening
Limited transparency on account minimums, maintenance fees, or membership requirements
best_for: Lubbock, Texas area residents seeking full-service banking and lending from a not-for-profit institution
Homebuyers interested in diverse mortgage options including FHA, VA, and USDA loan programs
Credit union members who value reinvested profits and community-focused financial services
Small business owners in the service area requiring commercial banking and loan solutions
diagnosis: ALLIANCE Credit Union is best for West Texas residents seeking traditional full-service banking with not-for-profit member ownership and competitive rates. The critical caveat is geographic availability—this credit union appears to serve only the Lubbock area, making it inaccessible for consumers outside that region.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
